TextENGNew: ...Now then.
This long, long journey — so long I can no longer recall clearly when it first began — has at last arrived at the hour of its ending.
Perhaps you feel it was not enough...?
Or are you satisfied with this...?
Hee hee... but from within this world of fragments, depending on how you fit the pieces together, you can reach any future — and even reach into the past.
If there is some other form you still desire... you'll have to go looking for that path yourself.
...Well, but that's beside the point.
The fragments that had scattered were at last gathered into one, and Rika Furude crossed the wall of June 1983 and stepped forward into a new future.
What kind of trials and hardships await her beyond that... I no longer know.

But I believe that the experience of facing down fate alongside her friends, the feeling of having overcome every obstacle with someone beside her — these things will be a great source of courage in the future she forges for herself.
So no matter how high the mountains that await her may be — no matter how many of them —
and no matter how fathomlessly deep the valleys that open before her may be —
...it is merely the price one pays for happiness. A labor, and a small one at that.
This is Rika, after all. I believe she'll find a way through.
And yet.
Where there is light, there is darkness.
And the brighter the light burns, the darker and deeper the shadow born from it grows.
Rika was able to grasp happiness.
...But what about the others?
For the villains, at least, this ending can hardly be called a happy one.
Their hatred and resentment will go on smoldering, never extinguished — until some spark sets them ablaze again... a malice like black fire that may consume everything within reach.
And if that malice were to wound the happiness she has only just claimed... to shatter it — that would not be at all surprising.
changeReason: Old says 'it could harm their happiness'; new says 'wound the happiness she has only just claimed... to shatter it.' The old uses generic 'their' while new specifies it's about Rika's happiness ('she'), changing the referent.

TextENGNew: ...The desire for happiness is a longing everyone carries by nature.
Even those who choose a life of hardship are, in their way, seeking spiritual fulfillment.
...So perhaps it is simply in our nature — human beings can never wholly abandon their ego for as long as they live.
And as that ego takes shape, it collides with others', sparks friction... and in the end, we wound each other.
When forced into a form unlike what we truly wanted, we suffer. We agonize.
When grief and anger compound, at last we begin to hate — to resent.
And so conflict is born.
In the worst of cases, one might reject the other's very existence entirely... whether the quarrel be large or small, grave or petty, the potential for serious strife cannot be denied.
To quell the kindling of such conflict... what in the world should one do?
...Yes, that's right.
It sounds simple. And yet it is exceedingly difficult.
Some people can talk it through and reach understanding. Others cannot.

Especially when their beliefs and values are at odds — they may shed blood, wound each other, and even knowing the pain and suffering that comes with it, there may be things they simply cannot come to understand.
...And yet, people cannot live alone, ignoring one another entirely... we are a terribly fragile and troublesome kind of existence.
That is precisely why, when you find yourself in conflict with someone... take a small moment to imagine what might be in their heart.
Why do they think in ways so incompatible with yours?
Why can they not understand what you are feeling?
changeReason: Old says 'Why can't you convey your feelings to them?' (failing to communicate outward); new says 'Why can they not understand what you are feeling?' (other's failure to understand). TextJPN 想いをくみ取ってもらえない means 'they can't understand/appreciate my feelings.'

TextENGNew: When you hold those questions in your mind, you step, just a little, into the other person's place — and from there, you may be able to look back at yourself.
Is that reflection... truly without fault?
Are you one hundred percent in the right — seeing things from a vantage point more elevated than the other?
changeReason: Old says 'Is it 100% correct, when viewed from a neutral point of view?'; new says 'Are you one hundred percent in the right -- seeing things from a vantage point more elevated than the other?' TextJPN asks if you see from a more 崇高な (elevated/noble) viewpoint than your opponent, not a neutral one.

TextENGNew: Of course, this does not mean acknowledging that you are inferior to them... or that you are the one who is wrong. That is not what it means at all.
It simply means you have considered, for just a moment, the circumstances of the other person... and let that consideration shape you.
So there is no need for self-deprecation. No need for guilt.
Simply — it would be kind to treat the other person with a gentle heart.
If you can do that, I believe the world will look just a little brighter than before — and the burden you carry on your shoulders will feel just a little lighter.
...Ah, it seems my time to say goodbye has come.
From here, I will cross into another world... and find my way to yet another someone.
changeReason: Old says 'to once again wind up with a certain someone'; new says 'find my way to yet another someone.' 'A certain someone' implies one specific person; 'yet another someone' implies a new unknown person -- different implication.

TextENGNew: Whether I will be able to help them, or whether I will cause them even greater suffering... hehehe — well, now.
I suppose I'll think about it once I've seen who it is.
Well, then. Until we meet again.

Someday, somewhere, I look forward to the day I meet you again...

Frederica Bernkastel
